wiki.summary: "Kingslayer" is a song by Bring Me the Horizon featuring guest vocals from BABYMETAL. Produced by Oli Sykes and Jordan Fish, the song appears on the group's 2020 commercial release POST HUMAN: SURVIVAL HORROR. Sykes initially came up for the idea of the song while playing Call of Duty in Amsterdam, with the Kingslayer being what everyone wants to be. He used this idea as an analogy for US president Donald Trump calling Antifa a "terrorist organization" Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "Kingslayer" is a song by Bring Me the Horizon featuring guest vocals from BABYMETAL. Produced by Oli Sykes and Jordan Fish, the song appears on the group's 2020 commercial release POST HUMAN: SURVIVAL HORROR. Sykes initially came up for the idea of the song while playing Call of Duty in Amsterdam, with the Kingslayer being what everyone wants to be. He used this idea as an analogy for US president Donald Trump calling Antifa a "terrorist organization" and the chaos caused by the Extinction Rebellion with Sykes feeling that people have to go out and do what they feel is right, no matter the cost. Hence the themes and for "Kingslayer" to break the barriers of the reality. BABYMETAL originally recorded all of their parts in English before Sykes asked them to re-record in Japanese. Bring Me the Horizon's lead vocalist Oli Sykes described the song as "sound[ing] like an anime TV trailer." The two bands toured Japan together in October 2023, performing "Kingslayer" throughout the tour. A live music video was released for "Kingslayer" composed of footage from the Tokyo performance on 14 December 2023. Bring Me the Horizon and BABYMETAL would perform the song live together again at Sick New World 2024. Sykes would go on to compare the sound of the song to something off their second studio album Suicide Season, and how the cute vocals of BABYMETAL on the chorus would make for a perfect contrast on "Kingslayer". Despite not being released as a single, the song has become a critical and commercial success, charting at no. 51 on the UK Singles Chart and receiving sales certifications in several countries Read more on Last.fm.
